With a Long Spoon — Chronology

Pre-Novel

Pace Meets Randi

Not a scene Event ~Sat Aug 8 day pre-novel
notes

Pace walks up cold to Randi's Greek group, gets accepted within fifteen minutes, asks her out inside that window. Genuine attraction, not strategy. The "He's hard to say no to" line originates here among witnesses. Social-penalty thread begins.

Three Weeks In

Not a scene Event ~late-August month pre-novel
notes

Pace and Randi have been sleeping together ~3 weeks before the Bench. Relationship established but not named. Cadence is the greedy early phase — frequent, weekend-overnight-heavy (weekday afternoons besides) — costing Randi the sorority weekends she'd been collecting standing on, and leaving her sore (past tense; resolves once Vee absorbs the weekends). Sorority house begins to converge on the view that a senior sister dating a social outcast is wrong; Brooke rides the consensus. (Backstory; see meta-arch-randi.md, The Cadence.)

---

◆ VOLUME ONE — Fall · the Beginning — the ascending relationship, through the first crack

Early

The Bench

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Sep 4 day Early fall week 1 the-bench.md detail
notes

Opening scene, Pace's POV. Their domestic ease rendered; BDSM sequence unhurried, tidal; she begs in a voice she hasn't used since childhood. In his bathroom afterward Randi practices the smile back onto her face. He holds her blind to the hunger he has inaugurated. Plants: clamps (held for Vee track); the hum in her ribs.

Standards

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Sat Sep 5 day Early fall week 1 standards.md detail
notes

Randi arrives home to Brooke at the chapter laptop. Four minutes that cost Randi the frame she's used to owning. Introduces Brooke; installs the social-penalty thread. Rhymes the Bench's bathroom-mirror beat.

The Pointing Game

Draft complete ↗ Scene 2026-07-12 ~Tue Sep 8 day Early fall week 2 weekday afternoon the-pointing-game.md detail
notes

DIPTYCH. Pace POV throughout, one continuous afternoon. Panel one (bed): post-coital, he draws out her fantasy; orgasm at "Would you like to taste another woman?"; she edits the wanting down to "Only in a threesome, with you" in the warm afterglow. He sees the move, lets her keep the camouflage — the frame she built to hide in is the exact frame he needs to proceed. Panel two (campus walk): the same act in daylight under a game's costume. He points; she judges. Rotating typology — too expected, they go everywhere together, she'd be a project, the inward girl who gets a maybe (and a hard kiss without his mouth wiped, to teach her what the maybe costs), the running-the-room girl who gets "God, no." The coil sets in; she is shopping. At the dining-hall doors he points at Vee — copper hair, laughing with her whole body — and Randi's composure breaks for one breath: the loose afternoon walk arrested mid-step, the bare "Yes" before the editing catches up. She executes the engineered collision so precisely it is the redhead who apologizes. She comes back glowing and failing to hide it — her feet do a small happy thing on the brick. Her name is Vee, and they have a stats class together. Plan begins; two of three know.

See You Later

Draft complete ↗ Scene 2026-07-12 ~Wed Sep 9 day Early fall week 2 morning see-you-later.md detail craft
notes

DIPTYCH. Panel one: Randi drops into the aisle seat, runs the name-bit on Cassie (who flat-returns), plants the latent-variable mirror. Panel two: Chi Latte — content-evaporation beat; Vee keeps the feeling of being chosen, not the transcript. Parting hug (rung zero of the goodbye-kiss staircase). Cassie's "Goodbye, Vee" warning buried in the seam. Collapses three former coffee/class beats into one diptych.

Substitution

Draft complete ↗ Scene 2026-07-12 ~Thu Sep 24 day Early fall week 4 substitution.md detail
notes

Randi texts Pace their location openly (Vee sees it and reads it as Randi texting some boy); Cassie clocks the summons and goes still. Vee deploys Randi's name-bit on Pace; Randi answers with a bare "Randi." He helps, takes her number. Cassie clocks that he showed no attraction to Randi — "He looked at her the way you'd look at somebody's great-aunt" — the not-a-prospect look (full personhood, no desire), which Vee privately recognizes as the look men give Cassie. Pace does NOT dehumanize Randi; he sees everyone, wants only Vee. The reader (who knows Pace and Randi are lovers and that Randi engineered the meeting to hide it) is left unsure whether it's flawless discipline or genuine only-eyes-for-Vee. The chapter ends on Vee hoping he'll reach out (she gave him her number; she can't text first). Plan formally live.

The Long Way

Draft complete ↗ Scene 2026-07-13 ~Thu Sep 24, hours after Substitution day Early fall week 4 long-way.md detail
notes

First proper date, hours after the meet-cute — his forthrightness, not a boy's delay. Small safe opener — frozen yogurt, daylight, nothing that asks too much. Establishes the courtship register her mother would approve; builds the trust the staircase will later spend. Closes on the walk and the tart (your tart's holding up), no weekend-ask on the page: the follow-through invitation is deferred to the considerate Friday-evening text that opens May I Choose (prompt but no pressure to answer that night — the consent-architecture read, warm on first pass). The a man, not a boy verdict now lands there — Vee lays out the evidence (the other boys made her answer on the spot; he left it with her, like it could keep), coming sideways, ahead of her deciding to say it, and Cassie — who teases everything and here doesn't — puts the word to it: That's not a boy. That's a man. Division of labor: Vee does the seeing (the comparison is hers, from a history Cassie never saw), Cassie the clear-eyed, no-skin-in-the-game friend does the naming — which arms the irony (even the Cassandra is charmed; a true-but-incomplete read that detonates later). Never an internal verdict; body before mind.

Water Wings

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Sep 25, after The Long Way, before May I Choose day Early fall week 4 water-wings.md Vee POV Vee + Cassie detail craft
notes

A warm Vee-and-Cassie afternoon at the university pool, doing three quiet jobs: a non-mother source for Vee's body being "too much," the involuntary anchor of the desirability thread (neutral third parties want her while she does nothing to invite it — the objective proof Pace and Randi can't give as compromised witnesses), and the setup for the Fitting (the leer-wound Pace's non-leering gaze later dissolves). Deck vs. water: the body unhideable in a one-piece, the world's "too much" — whistles, a holler, her practiced armor; the wish to be "sleek like Randi" rendered as wanting to disappear, never ranking herself lower. In the water, homecoming, not escape: she loved the water before she had a body, and the same abundance that draws the leers is what exiled her from synchro (the ideal her build couldn't use) — she lost the sport, not the water; the fade carries the first love and a thread of grief. Five beats: Cassie grumbles into the required suit (indifferent to exposure — the contrast) → the body as the world's "too much" → Cassie pops it with the dry objective seal → she sinks into the first love, the grief under it → Cassie splashes her out, Vee splashes back laughing (the water as play, alive and shared, fade). Leers stay ambient (a crossing reads creepy — the specific bid lives at Jitterbug); the exile is resonance, never exposition; no prowess (protects The Deep End). Anchor of the desirability thread (meta-note-desirability-thread.md).

May I Choose

Draft complete ↗ Scene 2026-07-13 ~Sat Sep 26 day Early fall week 4 the second date detail
notes

The first surrender. Safety assembled brick by brick at a Middle Eastern restaurant until her lifelong self-monitoring goes quiet and she goes under. The drip triggers it; mint tea surfaces her gently. The kiss is the surrender's second occurrence, elected. Plants: hand-motif, breach-verb ("let the thread go"), the may register deepened.

Off Six-Fourteen

Draft complete ↗ Vignette 2026-07-14 ~Sat Sep 26 day Early fall Cassandra track off-six-fourteen.md detail
notes

Same night as May I Choose, Vee comes in lit up and tells Cassie mid-recount. The clean baseline in the truest register, before Randi reshapes the material. Agency spine: she claims the kiss as her own act (I made him stop… I did it. Me.); the drink-question establishes she chose it sober. Plants the too-fast yes to hiking (primary placement), the who-is-he answered as a road-number "dossier," and the class question (rich people hike, poor people walk). Closes warm; the dread is structural, never on anyone's face. Distinct from Rye, the later, deepening telling after the hike.

Dear

Draft complete ↗ Scene 2026-07-14 ~Fri Oct 2 day Early fall stats class day SATC dear.md detail
notes

First SATC scene. Sisters approach and tax the friendship; Vee is marked as the cost Randi chooses to pay. Randi closes ranks — pre-jealousy, so purely loyalty-and-asset-protection. Result: brunches move private (off-campus, without comment). Recodes the brunch's privacy as concealment on reread.

Leave No Trace

Draft complete ↗ Scene 2026-07-15 ~Sun Oct 4 day Early fall turning their third date leave-no-trace.md detail
notes

First hike: short steep climb through a tunnel of trees to a black-rock summit over a silver lake; sudden cold rain on the descent. Borrowed ill-fitting boots → the blister beat (low-stakes cutting-voice-defusion install; elevation geometry prefigures the Fitting hem). Concrete summit lunch (rye/horseradish, shared water, cherries; the leave-nothing beat — second defusion install). Silk-naming on the trail: she names the russet, brick-red induction dress from a magazine — plants the silk Pace offers a variant of at the Fitting (he honors the want-to-be-beautiful beneath the color; he does not impose). "That sounds like a beautiful dress" plants beautiful. In the truck he gives her his only dry (no-tag, gym-bag) flannel and steps into the rain for her privacy; she beckons him back and, facing him, strips off her wet shirt — the shame-arousal loop's first full-force install — and gives him the sight of herself as a deliberate gift; he receives it without touching her. She feels him shivering; care overrides arousal; she sends them home. Fire-lane drop-off cut short by a campus cop's siren squawk. Builds the trust the later escalations spend. Keeps the no-tag shirt (detonates at the Fitting).

Rye

Draft complete ↗ Scene 2026-07-15 ~Sun Oct 4 day Early fall within a day of the hike, before the Fitting Cassandra track rye.md detail
notes

The pure romantic beat. Vee tells Cassie about the hike, the kept shirt, and the deciding, in the unperformed warm register Cassie can hold: the clean baseline of her feeling for Pace, before shame and before Randi reshapes the material, so the later contamination has something to contaminate. Distinct from the dinner-night telling (Off Six-Fourteen), the giddy first-kiss agency beat; this is the later, deepening telling after the hike. Photographic negative of Toenails: the romance Cassie can hold here, set against the bodily shame she cannot hold there. Cassie, who flagged Pace at the meet-cute, doesn't re-litigate.

The Fitting

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Sat Oct 10 day Early fall fitting.md detail
notes

He turned the heat up before she came. Burgundy silk matched to her coloring, not the magazine reference. No-tag shirt detonates. Measuring sequence close and rhythmic; the deliberate "Oops"; "Keep still" at audible cost. Ends with deferred tension intact — the tension that motivates the first night. Pays off: silk-naming (hike), no-tag shirt (hike). Plants: the deferred want.

Vee Tells Cassie About the Fitting

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Sat Oct 10 day same afternoon as the Fitting vee-tells-cassie-fitting.md detail
notes

Warmer, less performed than the Randi version. The heat detail lands hardest ("He turned it up days ago, probably"); Cassie goes still. Tips into comedy. Quiet Cassandra-track work. Pairs with the Cassie beat in Stats — Simpson's Paradox (experience → completed object).

How It's Done

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Sun Oct 11 day how-its-done.md detail
notes

First off-campus brunch — format upgraded (velvet alcove, Rombauer by name). Apparatus switches on. Two firsts: the first goodbye-kiss debut; the first ignition of Randi's jealousy via the water-glass tell on "he looked at me and I was fine." ← jealousy ignites here. Carries the first scent-name beat: Vee notices Randi's perfume, unnamed, in the pre-kiss closeness (flag 17).

First Night Together

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Oct 16 day first-night.md detail
notes

Motivated by the Fitting's deferred tension, not the CW dance. Pace invites her the morning he leaves for a three-day University of Chicago conference — "Dinner Friday?" — so she waits knowing the date. She arrives before the heat (mise-en-place ready, no flame). "I never thanked you for the shirt" opens the kiss. Fra diavolo and Chianti Classico become the meal after, not before. "Would you be bothered if I was too hard?" The trade: she folds the green satin panties in thirds, wet spot centered, drops the sheet, hands them to him bare. Best sex of her life; no management gap. Pays off: Fitting's deferment, the shirt-sleeping, the shame-arousal loop fully run. Plants: the scar (inner thigh, surgical — returns later), the gap that reopens in daylight retelling (First-Night Debrief), the green-sheets beat (undocumented future).

Toenails

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Sat Oct 17 day toenails.md Cassandra track detail
notes

Vee tries to tell Cassie and can't. Shame meets the wrong-shaped listener; Cassie can hold romance but not the bodily response. First withheld thing. The vacancy this opens is exactly what Randi fills at First-Night Debrief. Photographic negative of Rye.

First-Night Debrief

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Mon Oct 19 day stats class day the first class day after First Night — she can't wait to tell it SATC off-campus first-night-debrief.md detail
notes

Vee arrives carrying the floozie-shame she couldn't give Cassie. Randi catches the flush at maximum exposure and converts it to pride/aliveness — genuine kindness that is also the threesome on-ramp. Post-Fitting/post-jealousy: deliberate operation, so no tell. First read: the glamorous friend talked me out of my shame. Them-not-you seed and bathroom-trip beat migrate here.

Dress Pickup

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Thu Oct 22 day mid-week detail
notes

Lean scene, quick build to quick release — the bridge is crossed (First Night), so the heat is joyful and frictionless by design (vary heat by kind). Surprise mid-week invite she reads as a sex invite; he breaks off the about-to-be-sex to show her the finished gown, hung in the sun-porch window where he fitted her, the pedestal moved before a new mirror. Shared unveiling (happy tears) → private try-on, two risers (the bias and low chest; then a step opens the hip-high slit on her bare leg, shame crossing into heat) → she slides off her underwear and strides to him bare under the gown — her first initiation; he proceeds, tender; cut at the threshold. Coda: afterglow, the dress hung mid-passion, Pace asleep (up all night finishing it), Vee imagining the induction. Fresh shame occasion: not her body's involuntary tells but her own deliberate act, owned as heat. Ends on the unshadowed high. Pace has given Vee a mid-week slot — not a transfer from Randi (weeknights were never hers) but his feeling overflowing the weekend plan, a quiet tell of the greedy early phase.

Stats — Simpson's Paradox

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Oct 23 day morning after Dress Pickup stats class day stats-simpsons-paradox.md detail
notes

Absorbs the old standalone "Vee Shows Cassie the Dress." Vee comes to stats glowing and late off the overnight at Pace's (the gown home at the dorm, zipped, shown to no one) and sits between her two confidantes (Cassie | Vee | Randi). The dress is old news to both (the fitting tellings), so neither re-reveals it; the fork is in how each meets the transformed Vee. Cassie watches Vee, not the dress — "Was it a good night?" / "Yes. Oh, yes." Randi hears the other thing in that second yes ("You lucky slut"), draws out the shoe-gap, and binds it with a favor ("How's Saturday"), setting up Shoe Shopping. The professor's Simpson's-paradox drone bookends it — short-term trends reversing against the whole, same data, the total handing you the reverse of the truth — and Vee transcribes it, connecting it to nothing. Pairs with Vee Tells Cassie About the Fitting (experience → completed object, Cassie's Cassandra role).

Shoe Shopping with Randi

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Sat Oct 24 day SATC detail
notes

The showing-to-Randi contrast against the Cassie register in Stats — Simpson's Paradox. Triple-layered: Vee delighted / Randi running appraisal-as-interest sadism / reader watching the physical installation happen in plain sight. Inversion-rhyme: two people kneeling before Vee — Pace making something for her, Randi selling a version of her. Vee makes Randi laugh — genuinely, unperformed; on first read this is the friendship at its best, on reread it's the manipulation wearing the best disguise. Randi's hands on Vee's feet and lower legs while fitting shoes: subtle physical installation, deniably practical. Plant here: when Randi sees something she wants, she buys it — an impulsive mid-scene acquisition Vee clocks fondly. Pure characterization now; load-bearing in spring (grain #2's cover). Realized as a Vee's-room→boutique diptych: the gown's first unveiling to Randi, then the boutique fitting; Vee's credit-card transgression (most she's ever charged, no plan) against Randi's careless multiple. Played straight — the dark is reread-only; no scrunchie layer (that's spring).

Rock

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~late October month a weeknight stay post-sex, the porch after dark Vee POV detail craft
notes

The "who are you" talk every new couple has — food/home, born where, how many siblings, what parents called them, how he got to college at fifteen — under his quilt on the porch couch after sex: Vee in Pace's robe, Pace in boxers, herbal tea on the low table, den fireplace behind them, the mountains and the stars through the dark glass. The porch is his second liminal space: truth surfaces here calm and unforced (the structural opposite of the SATC restaurants, where Randi extracts it). In the easy flow of it his real name surfaces — Peter — the first time it touches the page. Not extracted, not a confession; it comes out because the porch is where he's calm, and she's allowed to hold it. He gives it with a faint set-aside (someone he put down, not someone he hides); the self under the name stays withheld (never explained). She tries it softly and he lets it sit — Peter becomes the name permitted here, in the calm. Banked, never stated: the porch faces the rock of the mountains, and Peter means rock — the foundation, surfacing in the one room that looks onto bedrock. Sets up the regression at First Weekend Back: she reaches for the porch-name on the cold step in spring and it won't travel (first use is here, warm — the spring reach is a regression, not a debut). Carries the scar's Step-1 deferral, small and under the name: in the same who-are-you flow she asks about the scar she's traced since First Night, and he closes it gently — the one thing he won't hand over even here, deeper than the name, paid off in spring (The Scar; meta-note-scar-reveal.md). Plants a light food-as-relationship seed — her childhood food made with love and plentiful but never quite enough, praised to Pace as his cooking but meaning him — that December's Porch Scene pays off with the crackers-and-meal figure (held back here). Taste thread: carry nothing (too early — the first gates want quieter hosts, and the name owns this scene). Low heat, tender register.

Lesson

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Thu Oct 29 day lesson.md detail
notes

"Don't think, just feel the lead" — plants the threesome's phrase. Erroneous proprietary jealousy (she has standing now, which makes being wrong worse). The older woman's correction: external corroboration against the sorority whisper campaign.

The CW-Dance Debrief

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Oct 30 day morning after the CW Dance SATC counsel register Randi's single cw-dance-debrief.md detail
notes

Vee already knows she was wrong — the older woman's correction landed complete Thursday night; the overnight was penance, not processing. She shows up at Randi's door Friday morning needing counsel, inverting the usual power geometry. Central irony: she is confessing to the woman Pace is sleeping with that she misread Pace as a womanizer. Randi's validation is eyewitness-credible and perfectly self-serving. Counsel register (not extraction — distinct from First-Night Debrief and the booth format). Randi steers Vee toward a full-throated apology without reservation (the posture Randi cannot occupy herself), reframes the jealousy as proof of feeling, and runs a wardrobe consultation from Vee's own closet — their bodies too different to share clothes, but shoes cross the gap. First shoe loan — Randi selects from her private archive of what she wore to Pace; the history rides along unspoken. Establishes the recurring pattern that culminates at the threesome. Vee's addition: the Humble Pie (store-bought, handmade label — her own idiom, not Randi's prescription). Social cost beat: a sister seeing the unaffiliated outsider at the sorority house threshold feeds Social Price #1.

Sheri — First Ride

Draft complete ↗ Vignette unreviewed ~Sat Oct 31 day morning sheri-first-ride.md Pace/Sheri detail
notes

Pace and Sheri on bicycles — the first rendered instance of a friendship that has existed throughout, and the one bond shown where the chivalric grammar simply works. Sits between the CW-Dance blowup (Thu) and the reconciliation that evening: Sheri, a CW regular, saw the redhead blow up at Pace Thursday and kept an eye on her the rest of the night. Now she asks. Pace doesn't lie to her; what leaks is affect, not the architecture — Sheri sees how gone he is. Learning they're together, she asks "What happened to the pale sorority chick?"; the non-denial earns "If that's not already complicated, it will be." Cassie warns Vee; Sheri warns Pace — in plain, uncoded language, the one register he can parse, and still can't act on. Brief, warm, Pace's register on the outside of his own architecture.

A Recognized Method

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Sat Oct 31 evening → Mon Nov 2 morning day recognized-method.md detail
notes

Vee arrives Saturday evening to apologize for the jealousy at the dance — the apology is the door-opening beat, shame arriving on his threshold. He takes it; she stays the night. Scene proper is Sunday, she wakes past noon: she's light, forgiven, spunky. Mouthy enough to earn one playful swat — the look crosses her face; she immediately initiates sex. Nobody names it. Plants the what was that look? hook. We Find Out is the deliberate escalation: recreation, naming, denial, physical proof. Movement III (Monday morning, a second unplanned night: the class scramble, her hair a fright with nothing of hers to fix it, his "You're always beautiful") plants the hair-need We Find Out answers and seeds the wearing-him domesticity; the bag-decision stays reserved for We Find Out. See meta-note-recognized-method.

The Practice Room

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Mon Nov 2 day the Monday she leaves his, after stats SATC music practice room practice-room.md detail
notes

First SATC scene with genuinely transgressive shame content. Container: soundproof music practice room — not the velvet booth. "Your turn" — the taking-turns ritual is already running; Randi moves directly to extraction. Vee's surface version (ha ha, he swatted my butt) peeled back to the real thing: the look, the immediate wanting, the charge that fired. Shame circuit fires in the retelling. Then — after the extraction, not before — Randi's spanking story as amplifier and installer ("If you think a swat is good, a spanking is better"): theatrical, explicit, porn-generic register, NOT Pace-fingerprint. Vee excuses herself to the bathroom; reason is specific — wetness, physical evidence her body produced without permission. Tempted to masturbate; doesn't. First bathroom trip; quick suppression. Randi clocks when she left. On return: "I hope everything is alright." — arch, deniable, devastating close. Pays off: A Recognized Method's look and unnamed wanting. Plants: Randi has now extracted the swat's effect, installed the escalation, witnessed physical arousal in retelling, filed the timing, and named it deniably — everything "Are your fingers sticky?" collects on at the We Find Out debrief.

We Find Out

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Nov 6 day the Friday after The Practice Room we-find-out.md Vee POV detail
notes

The deliberate escalation of A Recognized Method — the proof of her arousal, with the scrunchie provision folded in: recreation, naming, denial, physical proof — and the night the relationship's sealed-room engine first runs. Vee texts first for the first time (I miss you, can I come by early?) and comes early, before he's showered off the gym; she provokes without meaning to, he turns it to a game, he catches her. Several spanks confirm the look; "You enjoy this"; her denial (permanent armor, to him and herself); the wager (either you admit you lost, or we find out — his consent-gate in disguise); the finger goes in too easily, drawn out coated, received like it was true, like it was fine, like it was hers and good (rhymes the tree at First Night). Bet collected with ceremony and her agency — bare bottom she takes off herself, kneeling, sensitized, nothing like Randi's porn-generic story; denials audible as her hips lift. The sealed-room hinge: the sex rhymes the finger (too easily, all of him); the world contracts (shame wants a witness, and there was none) — the sanctuary that is the orchestrated stage, reread-only; see meta-thesis. Unplanned aftercare (juice, head in lap, wonderful, treasured); wakes hungry, the curry (class texture; plants the stay-in pattern behind the later why-never-out fight). Scrunchie provision folded in: the form on the sink — deep green with small white polka dots, distinct-but-not-custom, named once — read as care and duration; calibrated for the spring gaslight (meta-plan-scrunchie-gaslight). Overnight-bag habit originates here (the mirror decision); cements by The New Ordinary.

Vee Tells Randi About We Find Out

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Mon Nov 9 day within a few days of We Find Out vee-tells-randi-we-find-out.md SATC detail
notes

Vee tells more than she means to — the pantsing, the bet, he checked, the spanking — the penetration truth surfacing on her face before she can dress it. Randi answers with her own mixer night as amplifier (self-pleasure in a stranger's bed; porn-generic register, not Pace-fingerprint). First restroom self-touch, stopped before completion: at the first give of her own hand Randi's imagined fond gaze arrives — not the thinking, the looking — she can't be looked at like that and keep her hand where it is; she blots and carries the ache back (a second pulse). On her return, Randi's "Sticky fingers?" drops the floor out of the room — named-and-seen — then the deniable off-ramp: Randi's only nodding at the pastries that came while Vee was up, and Vee can't tell naming from innocence. Goodbye-kiss lingers past a friend's; the capstone — glaze passed to Vee's lip, sweetness from a pastry she had never once touched — advances the goodbye-kiss staircase and seeds the taste thread (meta-note-taste-thread).

The Top Drawer

Draft complete ↗ Vignette unreviewed ~early November month the weekend after We Find Out, into the next three movements the-top-drawer.md Vee POV detail
notes

Territory answering an abundance — the domestic staircase's territory riser, after the scrunchie provision folded into We Find Out; the domesticity accretes as rhythm, not a packaged beat. Movement one — the bed: drifting to sleep after a whole-day overnight (the door-lift, the foyer, a simple supper, the second time), the unshadowed early-love high — she admits to herself she loves him and reads it back only in his deeds (he says it in everything he does and names it in nothing), and says Peter aloud to him asleep, the true name voiced in the one moment it can't be received (unglossed; porch-permission from Rock). The afterglow carries the curvy-not-heavy beat — weight quietly come off, only bras and fitted underwear loose, the rest bought a size up because the racks misread her build (meta-craft-vivienne). Movement two — the mirror: weekend morning, hair up in his robe; he comes in behind her, his eyes find her scatter on his precise counter, and he says nothing. A light, non-maternal worry (a guest's wish not to be a trouble to a contained man), not the deep shame; she carries did he mind? to the next visit — the actions-vs-words question in miniature. Movement three — the discovery, the next weekend: the counter cleared (half a second of dread), then the drawers — his things squared down into one, the top drawer given to her, her form and pins, room to spare. Dread inverted into territory; the gift visibly cost him his own order. He never touches it again; the founding act is the only time his hand changes it. The drawer the spring grains run through. The week-apart that opens it is forced (his conference travel, her brutal week on campus), not a settled schedule — the greedy phase is still running, and School Nights intercalates in the internal gap (after the mirror, before the drawer). The regular pattern doesn't bank until The New Ordinary.

School Nights

Draft complete ↗ Vignette unreviewed ~mid November month the week between The Top Drawer's two weekends, after the mirror and before the drawer dorm, Vawter Hall Vee POV Cassandra track ~½ page detail
notes

The greedy phase given its only on-page trace — weeks in and still running, when they still can't keep to weekends (it doesn't bank into the regular pattern until The New Ordinary). Vee comes in lit up, fresh off the reunion weekend and the week apart before it (his conference travel, her brutal campus week); she relays the off-page weeknights in the romance/agency register, the appetite/shame walled off (the confidante-sort of Toenails, now running smooth) — though the wall isn't airtight: a tender deed she's proud to relay carries the body in unguarded (the burn treated gently, the warmth kept after). Cassie's dry "You don't come home school nights." Warm first read; on reread it's Pace's feeling overflowing the weekend plan (weeknights were never Randi's to lose), and Cassie — the Cassandra — keeping count of Vee's recession from her own life. Rhythm phrasing over a hard tally. Also the one natural place Vee sets Pace against the men before: Cassie's why are you so gone over this one? opens it, and Vee answers in deeds, not thesis — the men before were fine, Pace is not fine. Then Cassie asks it straight, do you love him?, and the talker goes small: "Maybe. I think so. It's all so fast." — not silence (which would read as duress) and not a declaration, but the giddy too-soon hedge, cold on reread against the plain she loved him she'd already owned to herself at The Top Drawer's bed. Keeps Cassie alive as the warmth/agency channel.

The Induction

Draft complete ↗ Vignette unreviewed ~mid-to-late November month a weekday afternoon, the ceremony hall the-induction.md Vee POV (Cassie present) detail
notes

The burgundy dress's public debut, and Vee's first chosen public display — the ownership beat (liberation as owning chosen shame, not shamelessness), played fully warm on the surface. Bare beneath it (the hip-slit shows the edge of anything, so worn as built it is worn bare), she keeps the slit managed all day — small steps, a hand at her hip while seated (foreshadows the hand on the hem at On Her Floor) — until the one walk she can't take small: to the stage for the scroll, where she steels herself, makes the second step long, and gives the room her leg to the hip on purpose, proud of it, the room working out there's nothing under the silk (a murmur goes through the crowd). The heat comes up as the body agreeing, not betraying — chosen shame owned, the thesis in miniature. Both orchestrators are on her body and neither is in the room: Pace's dress, Randi's dear four-inch heels (from Shoe Shopping). Pace is away at a mathematics conference in Berkeley (distinct from the Chicago trip in The Top Drawer) and quietly, un-performatively sorry — he made the dress for exactly this and misses the one wearing it was built for (harm-one: the fine man wouldn't have made it; Pace made it and ached to see it). Cassie is there as Vee's person and the witness — she catches the candid on Vee's phone (whole and thrilling here, uncropped), which Vee sends to Pace; it is the same image that reaches her mother in Cropped (the proud photo is secretly the wanting-photo). Vee's "How did I look?" / Cassie's "Like a movie star." On reread: the friend who documents the moment is the one the configuration will peel away (the last unclouded Cassie-and-Vee joy, between School Nights and Cropped); the being-seen-becomes-power she discovers here — contained, chosen — is what Randi later un-contains at On Her Floor. Register: psychologically intense, low-explicit. Nails: burgundy lightened to frosted plum (echoes the silk; matched at Shoe Shopping).

The New Ordinary

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~mid–late November range a Friday night, a couple of weeks after A Recognized Method new-ordinary.md Pace/Vee Vee POV detail
notes

The settled domestic rhythm shown rather than summarized, with the re-scenting beat staged inside it. They've gone several nights apart: the over-staying caught up with Vee (lost sleep, falling behind), and she reluctantly said she needed time for schoolwork; Pace didn't push (deference held genuinely, not as tactic) — which is how the greedy phase finally banks into the regular pattern (weekends his, weeknights hers; still running through The Top Drawer and School Nights, it settles here). Friday arrival: the lighter overnight bag now that the top drawer holds a few of her things (her space); warm greeting; straight to the bed, the reunion-hungry register — the ordinary baseline of how their sex is narrated, laid down deliberately and with specificity so the later taste-thread changes read as change, not as a flashing sign. After, the kitchen: Pace makes a from-scratch pizza in hyperfocus (breadmaker dough, a long tomato reduction, buffalo mozzarella, the stone and the peel), drinking a Nebbiolo sparingly because the work has him; Vee drinks more freely and watches. She marvels that she can taste the components — the difference between cotton candy and sliced strawberries. Taste-thread seed in the minor key: the food-safe practice run at connoisseurship, she doing the discriminating, Pace only naming the cheese ("the European kind"); rhymes forward to Whiskey and Wine and First Taste, points at nothing (meta-note-taste-thread). Then, unpacking, the shirt: she hands him the hike shirt washed and folded; he's puzzled, a little saddened (reads it as her giving it back). "This is still my shirt. You don't get to keep it." — playful, possessive, a defense of something precious — then, not quite meeting his eye: "I had to wash it. It doesn't smell like you anymore. I need you to wear it and give it back to me." He takes it without making her explain twice; his face splits with a warm smile — moved, and (untipped) just having learned how deep in him she sleeps: real-and-instrumental, never resolved. She'll have it back, re-scented, at the end of the weekend. The geometry felt, never named; the sibling-rhyme held (her things in his drawer, his shirt on her skin). Pays off: the washed-out shirt at the close of the CW Dance; the first-night trade.

In His Hands

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~mid-to-late November month the week before Thanksgiving break in-his-hands.md Pace/Vee Vee POV craft detail
notes

Warm and tender, low-explicit — the fall's gentler register, laid down before the spring turns transgressive. In bed after, Vee worn out, Pace takes comfort at her breast (the established comfort-motif), kissing her nipple; she feels him harden and, playful, "Again?" The exchange that gives the photo turns on his need to see her when she's away. The photo is the earliest, best-disguised seed of the sole-channel motif — his need to see her, and her own side given its due (she wants it; she likes how she looks in it because he does — the being-seen-through-his-eyes that pays off blindfolded at the threesome kiss). Vee picturing Pace alone with her image: tender, a little vulnerable, never shamed. Thanksgiving exchange: "What about Thanksgiving?" — he's cooking for Sheri ("She's alone"; Sheri offstage/reported, the absorber planted for the spring found-hair); Vee pushes on his family and gets the shadow — deflect, then one flat fact (I don't go back), the cause vaulted. Shadowless; his wound is warm-sad, not con-shadow.

All the Time

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~mid-to-late November month a few days after In His Hands all-the-time.md SATC craft
notes

SATC format-break — not an act-recount. Vee talks not about a thing they did but about the character of it: his attention, the athleticism, how often he wants her — the warm, kink-free baseline of their sex, and a change-up from the recount spine. The engine still runs: Randi extracts and names Vee's constitutional hunger as a continuous state (the crackers-and-meal — she's hungry, he's the meal), double register held (indulgent-friend warmth first read, the operator's appraisal on reread). Vee also shows Randi the plain nude photo from In His Hands — the one Gesso's painted-body photo later tops. Carries the anthro invite — Vee invites Randi to sign up for spring anthropology together (chosen, mirroring the accidental fall stats class; the spring binding; Cassie peeled off blamelessly — she's already done her humanities elective, with the oddball Dr. Wardy; her pre-nursing track locked with sciences). Anthro prof is Dr. Marsh (the easy section).

The Outlier

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Wed Nov 18 day week before Thanksgiving break (panel two); panel one earlier in the term outlier.md detail
notes

DIPTYCH. Panel one (stats class, earlier in the term): the professor assigns the 25% semester project — pick something that means something to you, real data, outliers, the exception proves the rule, exploratory data analysis. The three form the inevitable group and divvy the work (Cassie pulls the data, Vee — economics, the department's award — builds the model, Randi — marketing — makes the picture). Cassie's thesis (they didn't use it for workers) and her flat "my dad got laid off in 2020"; Randi believes her father's PPP loans saved jobs and means to prove it ("We'll see"). Panel two (library, week before break): plotting home counties, Randi drives into her own and the outlier resolves — MIRH → Miranda Holdings, LLC, her father's, several loans, the workforce cut anyway. She didn't know the extent; "Cheating bastards," recovery managed one beat too fast. Vee, beside her, sees the name, recognizes it, and says nothing then or after — kindness as silence; she never registers Randi as anything but a hurt friend (the operator stays reader-only). Cassie, who can't see the screen, delivers the true verdict blind — the exact indictment of Randi's father, to his daughter's face, not knowing it — inverting the usual power. No plan-acceleration here — this scene is pure character/relationship; Randi's acceleration into the plan is already carried by existing spring scenes (On Her Floor, Vee Tells Randi About the Bench, Ignition Scalding) and needs nothing from this beat. Silent stats-mirror: the professor's outlier/EDA method turned on the one who invoked it. Cassie's dad (laid off) and Randi's dad (the layoffer) are the two ends of PPP in one group — never stated.

Cropped

Draft complete ↗ Vignette unreviewed ~Sun Nov 29 day the Sunday back from break athletic-dorm cafeteria, Sunday dinner Cassandra track cropped.md detail
notes

A cropped induction photo turned her parents' pride into questions about the expensive dress and the man behind it; Vee fumbled "boyfriend (?)" and couldn't say Pace made it. Over dinner she gives Cassie the legible facts freely (math PhD, the hike, the dance, what she withheld and why), but the deeper question, what is he to you, stays the closed door: "Both. Probably both." The parents' table stays off-page, reported. Rhymes with Toenails: same wrong-shaped listener, different occasion. Opens Believe Me's emotional logic — Vee has now failed to name him truly, to her parents and to Cassie.

The Porch Scene

Draft complete ↗ Vignette unreviewed ~Sun Nov 29 day pre-dawn enclosed porch (weekend, the Sunday back) porch-scene.md detail
notes

Vee delivers the cracker-and-meal figure whole — her displaced I love you. He answers: "I love how good we are together" — sincere within his chivalric grammar, routing her speech down the sex-channel. She receives the warmth; underneath something begins to move. She later replays the sentence and finds the shapes don't match. She never tells Randi or Cassie. Plants the grain the arc then escalates across three displacements (Under the Rug → Bare → Believe Me).

Under the Rug

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Dec 4, after the Porch, before Bare day his house → restaurant → his house under-the-rug.md Pace/Vee Vee POV detail
notes

A triptych across three rooms — bedroom, restaurant, bedroom — in which a displaced fight is dissolved on the surface while the real thing is buried deeper; the false resolution is the point. Post-coital, the happiness wants the name his vow forbids; the want curdles, manufactured on the spot, into a just sex charge displaced through we never go out (pegged to the dance — the last time out). Pace's accommodation (let's go out) is the wall; the restaurant puts him in the uncontrolled sensory venue that wrecks him (rendered in behavior, neurodivergence never named — the reader learns why they stay in, and it was never shame); back home he refutes just sex through demonstrated care, not a declaration (vow intact); Vee relents — reprieve from fear, not grace, miscategorized to herself as forgiveness — and the makeup sex is warm and fully rendered (he loses control of everything but the word); the coda leaves nothing resolved — she belongs to him, with no name to hold him by. The knot: she refutes the truth with the truth. Motivates Bare's shapelessness going unbearable; the just sex displacement is the furthest of the arc's three (→ Bare, wordless → Believe Me, what are you to me). Runs entirely on fear, zero detective energy.

Bare

Draft complete ↗ Vignette unreviewed ~Fri Dec 11 day pre-dawn her room bare.md Vee solo Vee POV ~1–2 pages
notes

Vee alone, tossing and turning in his shirt. The shapelessness of what they are has been building since the porch — and was not released by Under the Rug's false resolution; in the dark it becomes unbearable; she takes the shirt off. No witness, no resolution. Second of the three displacements — wordless, alone.

Believe Me

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Dec 11 day evening the weekend before finals week pre-Christmas-break believe-me.md Pace/Vee Vee POV detail
notes

Vee brings her mother's Thanksgiving question to Pace — the one she couldn't answer then and can't answer now: what are you to me? She conflates monogamy and I love you. "Do any of your friends have a relationship you'd rather have?" — genuine within his grammar, insufficient within hers. No reunion — the pattern breaks here: where Under the Rug dissolved into makeup sex, this fight won't dissolve. She throws the gauntlet (the demand to be named); he answers warm-but-wrong and won't chase; they go silent — she won't retract, he doesn't sulk — straight through finals and the break to First Weekend Back, where she finally sets the demand down. Last of the three displacements — the want finally named and mismatched; the one that ruptures instead of papering over.

Fairytale

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Thu Dec 31 day New Year's Eve Christmas break fairytale.md Randi's POV — mirror aperture, load-bearing detail
notes

The conventionally proper man, the fine dinner, the fine sex, the nothing it produces. Randi at the bathroom mirror: the eyeliner holds because nothing reached her eyes; she rebuilds the face exact and there is no one behind it, registering the comparison. The choice enacted by the return to the bed. The chosen-not-to-throttle moment. Rendered from inside her under the containers rule (flag 15): second opening of the mirror aperture, rhyming with the Bench mirror — installation → confirmation. Placed on New Year's Eve, the same night as Old Acquaintances: the two women flat without him in parallel, joined only by the midnight text (Vee's open hand, Randi's fairytale gloss), neither aware of the mirror. Structural hinge: every scrunchie grain lands after this scene — the reader enters the spring holding the nothing and the choice already made, so what follows reads as a starving apparatus leaking, not malice.

Old Acquaintances

Draft complete ↗ Vignette unreviewed ~Thu Dec 31 day New Year's Eve Christmas break old-acquaintances.md Vee solo craft
notes

Listless and flat at home without Pace, without Randi. The absence of both in the same week is information she doesn't fully process. Her mother's world — the questions, the ordinary furniture of home — sits beside her strangely. She is somewhere else without knowing she's left. Same night as Fairytale: the two women flat in parallel, the midnight text the one seam that joins the panels. Plants the beige-life thread (meta-note-old-acquaintances.md): the flat, safe, unseen home rendered as the concrete cost of a life without Pace, and the book's bargain first weighed in her interior — the scale set here (First Weekend Back tips it). Sleepless in her childhood bed, the lavender-and-fabric-softener field inverting Bare's scent channel; the Gstaad postcard she envies (reader-hollow — Fairytale's emptiness). Objects chosen to be recallable; somewhere else without knowing she's left is the line the ending inverts when she chooses that life knowingly (the final Cassie scene).

The Usual

Draft complete ↗ Vignette unreviewed ~Thu Dec 31 day Christmas break, after Believe Me the-usual.md Pace/Sheri craft
notes

Winter kills the ride; the meal is the scene. A backroad diner, a booth face to face — the overshare loosens through easy, cost-free company and food, not any no-eye-contact form (the old "counter spares him" rationale is retired — he is introvert-charismatic, not autistic, and eye-avoidance would poison the decency of his having looked at Sheri through her own troubles). Pace and Sheri are the two with no family table over the holidays — she was disowned, he won't go back — and neither says so (carried wordlessly: the Leave It to Beaver family under the tabletop glass; the jukebox breaching the one refuge from the Christmas loop). Pace carries the question Vee left unanswered at Believe Me; asked to say the words, he answers her demand for a declaration with a ledger — love-as-accounting, his mother's wound — and cannot say why he can't: "Is that so hard?" answered only by "I can't." Sheri hands him the plain truth ("from this seat, that feels pretty shitty") and then lifts him back out of it — the scene ends warm. Pays off the first ride. Third panel of the break triptych with Fairytale and Old Acquaintances: three principals apart, but his is the only bond on the page that isn't lonely. Establishes Sheri as the one who sees Pace from outside his own architecture. New canon established here: Pace brought Randi to the CW dance hall once (~Nov) — see continuity flags.

The Stranger

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Jan 22 day morning the reconciliation day (beat 1 of 3) stranger.md Vee POV detail craft
notes

Door one of the two-door reconciliation, now its own beat. Off a late-night flight, the first thing she does is drive out to mend — and never gets past the stoop. Not a second fight (the what-are-we stays open all year — the Cabin key soothes it, the peaches reopen it): Vee reaching and hitting his cold wall, his cold the stress-tell, not a verdict. The "Peter" reach lives here and goes unanswered — she reaches past Pace to the true name, the I love you neither can produce; it lands for the reader but fails to thaw him. She drives away crying, the wanting broken by nightfall. The honest daylight reach, and it fails.

My Friend Randi

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Jan 22 day afternoon the reconciliation day (beat 2 of 3) Randi's single my-friend-randi.md Vee POV SATC/confidante craft
notes

The pivot, promoted out of the reconciliation-day gap into its own scene. Wrecked from the morning wall, Vee does what she did after the CW Dance — goes to Randi. The reunion after the break (both badly missed each other; only the midnight text bridged them): refuge, the confidante bond deepening. She vents the silence, the no-name wound, and the Sheri fear she never puts to Pace — and Randi coaches her: stop reaching for the word; show him what you are. The nothing-under-the-coat appeal is Randi's idea. The confidante track produces the act that saves the relationship — she makes with words what Pace makes with his body. First read: the boldest, most loving girlfriend advice. Reread: the real other woman soothes Vee's terror of the false one, then sends her back into the unnamed configuration — trade-the-word-for-the-body is the plan's thesis, spoken as love. Physical: a big, long hug (deniable — missed each other) with a flicker of too-long, Vee breathing Randi in deeply the way she does Pace (the scent channel opening, reader-side only, never cognized); the usual settled goodbye kiss closes it. Randi soothes the Sheri fear emotionally, without the fact — the experiential dissolve waits for Another Round. Full craft: craft.

Nothing Underneath

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Fri Jan 22 day night the reconciliation day (beat 3 of 3) nothing-underneath.md Vee POV detail craft
notes

Door two, on Randi's counsel. She puts herself together (long coat, heels, nothing underneath), and the whole approach is now the scene's engine, run on agency, not shame: at the mirror she turns the body she's hidden since fourteen to its purpose and denies herself the finish, saving it for him; a reckless drive with a police-cruiser false alarm that leaves her wound tighter, denied twice; the locked door and the knock, everything on one card. Pace says one distancing line (stress-tell, fear not verdict); she doesn't argue and doesn't reach for Peter (the demand set down, not the hope), answering the cold by opening the coat, wordless. Then the body-over-word inversion made literal: he takes her with his mouth on the cold step where his words failed that morning (seeding his relish for her taste → Forbidden Kiss), then a starved standing-to-couch fuck, reunion-ferocity inside already-crossed acts (no new threshold). He gives no answer (the vow holds all year — the Cabin key is his eventual object-answer, the peaches reopen the wound), but the tender afterglow restores her name ("make an entrance, Vee" — the morning's stranger undone) over midnight scratch pancakes, and the cloud lands as one submerged beat: his voice cracking on "I missed you so much" where the word would be, she too full to miss it (demand dead, hope alive). Coda: Sunday she's back in the dorm in her coat and his sweats and the heels, to Cassie's dry "Hmm." Volume One's close — the false reconciliation, the Beginning's last beat. (The public-echo bomber-jacket beat is its own scene, Among Friends, opening Volume Two.)

---

◆ VOLUME TWO — Spring · the Middle — the apparatus at full extraction; the bloom before the souring

Middle

Among Friends

Architecture complete Scene unreviewed ~late January month that same first weekend or a weeknight soon after (post–First Weekend Back) a low-key bar or TV-less place, Vee's choice Vee POV detail craft
notes

The first of the paired world-openings (Another Round reciprocates — Vee into his world) — Pace enters Vee's world, his deed-answer to the Under the Rug visibility wound (will we ever be seen in public?), given in deed, not word: he comes to a gathering of her friends without being asked, in his bomber jacket and broadcloth, and is seen with her. Pace proposes the outing; Vee — thrilled and nervous, set on not repeating Chili's — asks him to pick because he knows the area (the deniable cover for the real reason, unnamed), and he lands a roadhouse with food (over a campus bar; under-21 Kayla stays with the group) — his initiating and choosing the ground he can hold, her steering-via-excuse, both unspoken why; why not Chili's answered in silence (the un-named neurodivergence). Vee has assembled an unlikely mix — her own friends (dorm-floor Kayla and Meg, English-class Theo; seed, flag 22) + Randi (crossing status lines for Vee) + Pace — the connective warmth only Vee has, and the mechanism that unknowingly seats her boyfriend and her orchestrator in one room. Pace works the room, Randi included: the joke-setter who lets others land the lines. He and Randi hold the meet-cute line (met once), the concealment in uniformity, not distance — he charms Randi at room temperature; the absent thing is desire, not warmth; Cassie doesn't re-clock the no-attraction. Vee pulls Randi over to properly meet him (as far as she knows, they met once); she under-claims — "this is Pace" — and he completes it, "Vee's boyfriend," the one public label he'll give, said to his concealed lover's face: claim, met-once, and cut in one, Randi's hand closing hard around Vee's while Vee reads only happy for me. Answers the visibility axis in deed while the enclosure of his own life holds — the answer soothes and deepens the cage at once. Vee's POV arc: nervous (will her worlds mix?) → floatingly happy (claimed, her friends charmed, Pace attentive); one misfiring twinge watching Pace make Randi laugh — the Christmas Sheri-fear she'd confided to Randi in my-friend-randi, firing at the right category and the wrong woman while the real one laughs with her hand in Vee's. The bloom is load-bearing — dig Vee in deep so the spring/summer escalation reads as believable, not folly. Volume Two's warm opener — the bloom. See flags 22, 23.

Another Round

Architecture complete Scene unreviewed ~late January month after First Weekend Back and Among Friends before Grace a taproom Vee POV detail craft
notes

Vee finally meets Sheri — the first of Pace's people she's ever met, the sealed world cracked open a little (his people before his house — the Cabin key deepens it to dominion a month on). The three of them out for drinks; Sheri's testimony comes in the gaps, while Pace is up fetching rounds. Two parts, across two drink-runs: they met dancing and he was the one man who didn't get drunk or handsy (the dancing the point, not the prelude); then — drier, no self-pity — she came out, every inherited bond dropped her at once, and Pace stayed: didn't insult her with they'll come around, just kept turning up with bacon cheeseburgers after a ride. Sheri being a lesbian quietly dissolves Vee's proprietary brace; what's left is warmth. Told, then shown — the girls get drunk and Pace, sober, drives them both home. The book's best piece of Pace-decency, finally on the page through a witness instead of locked in author-knowledge: he never opens up, his goodness is testified. Reader-side and submerged — the realer his constancy, the louder the word he still won't say, and the deeper the trade Vee keeps making to feel it. Sheri stays in her dry, puncturing register; no diagnosis of Randi (one glancing ping at most). Surfaces the disowning on the page for the only time — revises the Sheri "never dramatized / Pace-POV-locked" discipline (see craft note). Reciprocates Among Friends (Volume Two's opener): her world opened publicly there, his sealed world cracks a notch here — outer access, then inner.

Forbidden Kiss

Architecture complete Scene unreviewed ~late January month just after First Weekend Back, before The Reach Vee POV detail craft
notes

Taste-thread gate #1 (the Refusal), on the page. After he goes down on her, Pace comes up to kiss her still coated — doesn't hide it, doesn't announce it; she's coming down from the orgasm, not tracking, and doesn't clock it until their lips touch and she feels the slick. She turns her head, seals her lips, wipes her mouth: "that's gross / I'm not gay" (the conflation — a woman's wetness in her mouth = gay = wrong — stays subterranean, never analyzed). He files it, wipes his face, they go on. Post-coital install: on her side holding him while he sleeps, she reaches back over the wiped kisses where a trace still got through — the retrospect retro-installs the Leak's whole Oct→Jan history (so First Night and everything since stay clean) and lights the conscious wondering (what am I missing, minor key). Opens the spring transgressive through-line: the Leak runs live and conscious through the spring sex scenes, mostly as gracenotes. Tone (governing, meta-note-taste-thread.md): warm on both reads, no sinister signal — the faint aimed-ness (his care contracting to the deniable minimum because this is the gate he most needs down) reads as the first grain of the consent-hole's year-long accretion, depth not dread, never a visible breach; the enabling genuinely is enabling. First read it's the smallest of busts.

Vee — The Reach (solo)

Unwritten Vignette unreviewed ~late January month Vee's room Vee solo
notes

Alone, masturbating, working from the memory of how he looked at her wet — like it was true, like it was fine. At the peak she brings her fingers to her face: the sniff she can do (the old animal-evidence flinch, now threaded with wanting); the tongue she can't — it stops a breath short, no contact, and she doesn't have the reason (the taboo she could name for his coated kiss has no purchase on her own fingers; the deeper stop has no words). She wipes them, finishes; the wanting-to-know outlasts the orgasm. An open loop, reaching for the knowledge of herself only he has. Sets up the cabin's watched version (private → witnessed) and First Taste (the loop he later closes). Taste held; the woman-taboo stays subterranean. Body before mind — she feels the wall, never names it.

Barely Stings

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~late January, after Forbidden Kiss, days before Still Life month barely-stings.md Vee POV (solo) detail craft
notes

The cold half of a cold/warm diptych with Still Life. Vee's first wax, at Pace's unexplained request — agreed with the noticed ease that is the submission (the fear stays private). Arrival (girls primped to be seen / to be touched — she's the second now); white wine, not champagne (functional, loosens the body not the conscience); a doctor-ish table, no stirrups; the tech friendly → severe (POV-colored). Verbal shame first: she must say aloud she wants her sex entirely bare. First strip / a curse (out of character); edges worked inward. The cold "Don't move" and the hand that grips or presses the thigh flat — controlling, functional, the wrong hand (it grips because it's working, not because it wants her; she gives the tech nothing). Submission is Pace's alone — no one to submit to here; the arousal is the offering with nowhere to land (bare for him, across his absence). Two indignities: the labia strips (peak pain — the body betrays her) → turn-over / anus unconsidered, shock, goes along (the will betrays her: compliance past her limit — barely stings writ large, Randi's curated omission; pure humiliation, not converted to Pace, charged not grim). Coda — three hands: alone, she touches the new smoothness, imagines what Pace will feel — the offering readied for Still Life. Randi in recall only — the rec tossed off after stats class at Chi Latte (in person, off-track; never a phone call): the good place, the esthetician by name, the warm lie — "it barely stings, you'll love it." It stings. Keep-still diptych with Still Life (told don't-move here / can't keep still there — the failure migrating from sting to arousal). Title = Bare + the lie: consent steered by a deniable untruth, the book's engine in miniature. Primes Gesso (the callout); feeds Still Life (the bare canvas).

Still Life

— Scene unreviewed ~late January, days after Barely Stings month Partial draft (still-life.md; through the gesso runaway — photo/sex/afterglow unwritten) Pace/Vee Vee POV detail craft
notes

The painting scene, now a taste-thread beat — playful going in, transgressive at the turn. Painted as fruit — grapevine (wrist→armpit→side of breast), cantaloupe (breasts), cherries (mons); he traces each route with his mouth first, then the cool brush, feeding her the fruit he paints her as (grape, then melon) so the build keeps breaking and she can't crest early. No peach (originates at the cabin blazon and seeds forward to peaches.md — planting it here pre-spends the July apex; counter peaches are texture only). Arranged as a cornucopia — she is the plenty, her body lush, abundant, overflowing (the "too-much" the world leers at, here turned to worship). Shame occasion: the failure to hold the pose — can't-keep-still, bodily responses, the overflow (her own excess, uncontainable) that becomes the medium. The runaway is the mons: he paints her in her own fluids, and the taste progression lands — she tastes the cherry, then wonders about the taste of what he's painting her with (post–Forbidden-Kiss wondering; taste held, never crossed here → First Taste; meta-note-taste-thread.md). The photo she wants — of her nude, waxed, painted, transformed body, to hand to Pace and later show Randi: an escalation of the plain nude photo from In His Hands, the sole-channel motif deepened (she likes how she looks only as he sees her). Origin of "adventure": the afterglow — head on his chest, both a paint-smeared wreck — Vee coins it here, "Well, that was an adventure," and helpless laughter; hers, joyful, offhand, Pace present to hear it, the word he later takes up as the cabin's invitation and that detonates at the threesome (meta-note-grace.md). Rides out unremarked, never a code.

Gesso — Vee Tells Randi

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~late January, a few days after Still Life month SATC detail
notes

Shows the photo — the painted, transformed body (an escalation of the In His Hands nude). "Gesso?" — Randi names the primed ground (the white base a canvas takes before the paint), her one word titling her own scene. The small smile already in place on return. She opens knowing the wax already happened; Vee calls out the barely stings lie, and Randi laughs — "I knew how much you'd love it; I wasn't going to let you skip the pleasure over a moment of pain" (both-tests: indulgent friend / consent edited "for your own good").

On Her Floor

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~Late January month on-her-floor.md FB detail
notes

Randi loans Vee a stretchy knit skirt for a sorority event. On Randi's floor, Randi recruits Vee into a show for Harrison: disco handholds, Randi's lead, skirt riding up, then a paused open-mouth kiss framed as play. Touch-signature and kiss-signature deepen under deniability.

Vee Tells Pace About the Kiss

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~early February month just after On Her Floor Vee POV detail
notes

She can't take the mixer kiss to Randi (naming it exposes her own want) and Cassie can't hold the bodily/queer charge — so she brings it to Pace, the only door left. Processing it turns hot; afterward, in the afterglow, he asks whether she's ever thought about being with a woman. The threesome plant — his, deniable, real curiosity AND the move at once; ends on the open question, nothing agreed. Motivates the still-unannounced Forbidden Kiss and gives the "I'm not gay" door-slam a concrete referent. Placed before The Jar so Randi's control of the Pace-channel leaks; what Pace conceals when Vee names Randi is charged and unstaged. See meta-note-threesome-reveal.

The CW Jitterbug Scene

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~Sat Feb 6 day detail
notes

She balks at the lifts (too heavy); he offers and waits at the consent line. Airborne escalation; shame-to-ease. Rehearsal-for-restraint — rhymes bondage and the blindfold. This is the Brooks and Dunn tribute night Sheri invites Pace to at The Usual (~late Dec) — he comes and brings the redhead, paying off her "you're dancing, and I don't care what the redhead's got to say about it"; Sheri is a CW regular and may be on the floor.

Vee Tells Randi About the Dance (The Jar)

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~Feb 10 day within a few days of the Jitterbug SATC detail
notes

The dance as the funny story. Then the jar — what his invisible chosen gentleness did to her. Voice changes mid-telling. Randi files the cruelest datum: tenderness/restraint is the load-bearing wall of what Vee feels. Off-page consequence (never marked in text): this is the witnessed-aliveness scene that detonates the drawer's first visit — Fairytale (both flames or no fire; nothing outside the configuration) → the Jar (Vee thriving openly on the exposure Randi can only endure — envy-of-capacity, the water-glass nerve, at full amperage) → the taking, on a weekday in the following weeks. The fuse is visible only on reread.

On Top

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~February month before Restrained Vee POV detail craft
notes

The first time Pace physically edges Vee — distinct from the SATC brunches (Randi edged by Vee, conversationally) and from Vee on the Bench's equipment-edging (~late May), which this introduces and Vee on the Bench later runs to apex. Light, playful register: she's astride him, and as she nears the edge he simply brings his thighs together to delay her, again and again. Vee discovers she both likes and hates it. Fresh indignity is agency-shame — her wanting becomes her own active motion; she buys each denial by grinding for it (different from Vee on the Bench's involuntary-announcement shame, so it doesn't pre-spend it). Taste thread: default carry-nothing (no oral foreground).

Restrained

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~February month detail
notes

Restraint removes the question of her hands — a gift for involuntary-response shame. Told to Randi (SATC); voice changes; "Comfortable?"

A Threesome, With You (The Conversion)

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~late February month a different day from the plant, after intervening brunches and solo-processing Vee POV
notes

The plant germinates off the page — SATC brunches and the spring nighttime vignettes (almost, then actually, masturbating to the idea she can't take to Randi) — and surfaces here as her move: she converts a woman into a threesome, with you — because Pace is what makes experimenting safe, and she's honest enough to tell him so. Vee-authored (this is what keeps Pace deniable; he never asks). Starts with Pace in the room for safety, not yet the fully-interactive shape — the move to Pace with the woman too is the Randi-steered spring escalation that lands at the Agreement. The safety she names here is the same safety that became conditional rent at First Weekend Back. Warm on both reads; the cold is the reader's. See meta-note-threesome-reveal.

Winter Wonderland

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~January–February range a snow day no fixed sequence (snow-window only) Vee POV
notes

A relationship beat in the cold — sex likely, but not the point. A garage sale they browse together, where Pace buys an old Flexible Flyer (wooden-slat, steel-runner sled) for their use → sledding on the hills above Blacksburg. Clumsy, warm, laughing-in-the-cold register; one of the "earn the dark by being light" outings. Possible diptych: the garage-sale panel + the sledding panel. Snow-window constraint: deep-winter weeks of the spring semester (Jan–Feb/early March).

Randi — Spring Inversion

Unwritten Vignette unreviewed ~mid-February month MUST precede the drawer's first visit (sequencing constraint) Randi's POV — room aperture Randi/sorority
notes

The sisters haven't noticed anything — she's been careful, keeping her Pace time to weekdays and coming back to the house each night to make morning class. The plan costs her nothing visible. What it costs is the weekends: Vee gets those. Randi gets the weekday scraps and has engineered this herself, for a girl she's certain she outranks. The invisibility of the sacrifice is the thing that burns — no one to argue with, no credit to claim, just the arithmetic she can't say aloud. Rendered alone in her single room under the containers rule (flag 15): second opening of the room aperture, rhyming with Standards — the managed life rendered → the managed life's invisible cost. Placement is load-bearing: this is the reader's last interior access before the campaign opens; the burn is shown, then the hand moves off-page. On reread the vignette is the visible fuse.

The Drawer — First Visit (off-page, never rendered)

Not a scene Event ~mid-to-late February month a weekday afternoon detail
notes

Randi takes ~6 scrunchies from the form (~38 → ~32) during one of her weekday visits. Never staged, never marked, in no character's POV — it lives in the seams of Pace's attention, inside or between written scenes. Do not plant a deniable "she excused herself" beat anywhere; the strongest version marks nothing — weekday access alone is the opportunity. Motive chain (reread-only): Fairytale → the Jar → the taking — the plan escaping her, not strategy. Preconditions already on the page: the overnight habit and the form (The New Ordinary, early Nov); Spring Inversion (the burn).

Grain #1 — The First Flicker

Unwritten Vignette unreviewed ~late February month an overnight at Pace's Vee POV mirror-time
notes

Vee at the drawer mid-ritual: is the form lower than it should be? A steeper drop than her own use explains — but down is the plausible direction, so it's unconfirmable, unsayable. The question arrives mixed into the post-Christmas-Fight stream (what am I to him, why won't he say it) so it reads as one more symptom of wanting definiteness from a man who doesn't issue it. Both readings stay open — anxious attachment or accurate instrument; the reader is given no more certainty than Vee has. Puts her half-consciously on watch.

Grain #2 — Worn in Plain Sight

Architecture complete Vignette unreviewed ~ongoing from March onward month recurring detail
notes

Randi wears Vee's scrunchies around Vee — and Vee is thrilled: the glamour queen is mimicking her style. The evidence lives in the open, filed under flattery — the loving-Randi cover, twin to grain #3's loving-Pace cover; every corrupted datum in the campaign gets explained by love. First instance spoken — Vee delighted ("did I actually convert you?"), Randi's double-register reply ("You've ruined me. I used to have standards."); recurrences unremarked, set dressing only. Late variation, post-Restoration: one sighting where the delight takes a half-second longer to arrive — the only trace her instrument is still running underneath. The reader, carrying the First Flicker, gets the jolt (wait — are those—?) while Vee beams; unprovable either way. The only mutuality Vee ever perceives in the friendship — at last something flows from her to Randi — and it's the theft.

Randi — Social Price #1

Unwritten Vignette unreviewed ~March month slot TBD when spring drafting begins (flag 16 window) Randi/sorority
notes

Chapter meeting, or Brooke again, or a sister's offhand comment — the social tax of Randi's choices made legible. She's missing events, losing standing, choosing the plan over the frame she used to own. She absorbs it without flinching. The plan is worth it; she's still in control. First visible depletion of the social capital she's been spending — the cost Spring Inversion held invisible, now surfacing in company.

Randi — Social Price #2

Unwritten Vignette unreviewed ~April month later than #1 slot TBD Randi/sorority
notes

The accumulated cost of months of absence — events skipped, standing not collected, the chapter's social fabric pulling away. Not a confrontation; a quiet audit. The plan has cost more than she accounted for. She's still choosing it.

The Burn

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~mid-to-late February month after Restrained (spaced from it), before Grace and the Massage Table burn.md Vee POV detail craft
notes

Vee into Pace's world (the reciprocal of Among Friends), and it goes wrong — vindicating the sealed room rather than opening it: the outside proves careless, his fury and tending draw her back to the sanctuary, felt now as more precious. At the gym where Pace powerlifts (182 class), he spots her squat; in the next cage, guys screwing around with an uncollared bar let a plate slide off and roll into hers. She flinches under load and wrenches her neck/trap; Pace racks the bar (lifts the danger off her body). His fury is cold, precise, leashed — never physical, hands off the kid (the powerlifter who refuses to be careless with his force is the man his father wasn't; same-shape resonance to the bike-dragging, never named, body before mind). Vee forgives; Pace stands down at her signal, body a beat behind his words (even his anger defers to her); the banked charge converts to the tending. Drive home, hot shower, therapeutic massage (Vee POV; his roughened hands read the soreness, competence only in what they do). The tending crests when she chooses the balm's burn on her own nipple over not being touched — her body chooses first, then she chooses, knowing the cost (the ownership hinge, wordless); his control breaks, licensed by her ("Keep still" landing as protection, then "I know, I know"). Then the kiss lights the wrenched neck — the body that wanted her into this forecloses the having — and he re-gathers: "we need to wait." He gives her the most her hurt body can safely take (one balm-hot hand, one clean, kept just under a release the neck won't allow); both stranded, the fire kept, "not yet" installed un-engineered. No sex (no intercourse); taste carry-nothing. The injury is why he'll buy the massage table (option a — Pace-interior, off-page; the planned Massage Table scene keeps its pure-gift register). See flag 24.

Into the Dark

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~February–July range before The Threesome and Reveal Vee POV
notes

A relationship beat — a day trip to a Virginia cavern (Dixie Caverns near Roanoke is the nearest real option; author's call). The two of them underground among the formations, the constant cool, the world above shut off; the outing and the closeness are the point. Symbolic of the blindfold — the going-into-the-dark-together, led where she can't quite see — a thematic prefiguring of the threesome's blindfold, not a literal blindfold beat; must precede The Threesome and Reveal.

Grace (The Pivot — the Key & the Watched Exposure)

Draft complete ↗ Scene unreviewed ~late February or early March month grace.md detail craft
notes

His answer to the Christmas fight, on his terms: a key to his house, in a handmade oiled-wood box (the answer he can't speak). The transgression is watched masturbation ("Would you show me how you touch yourself?" — she's made the agent of her own exposure); the Permission Rule installs (tell, not ask). "Adventure" first invited here (coined at Still Life). Taste held for First Taste (~late spring). Surprise takeaway to an isolated mountain cabin; he packs the trip, never the Top Drawer.

Cabin Debrief Brunch

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~early March month after Grace SATC debrief register
notes

Vee tells Randi she let him watch — the exposure she crossed. Randi receives confirmation the plan worked at its deepest level; steers Vee toward acceptance rather than shame or regret — genuine warmth that is also the coldest calculation so far. Goodbye-kiss staircase: the goodbye lingers a beat too long; Vee explains it away. First read: the glamorous friend talked me out of my embarrassment. Second read: confirmation received, staircase advanced.

The Deep End

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~early to mid March month
notes

A conversation about sports turns tactless: Pace calls synchronized swimming dumb. Vee did eight years of it. She's insulted; she invites him to the pool to show him. She demonstrates; he attempts it; total failure. The power dynamic briefly inverts — she's the expert, he's the clumsy beginner. The laughter dissolves the insult without requiring contrition he doesn't quite feel. Playful register; earns the dark before the next escalation. His remark is behavioral-portrait: strong opinions organized by his own criteria, stated without checking who's in the room. Title: literal deep end + Pace in over his head — out of his depth against her expertise, his mouth the thing that got him there.

Green Sheets — The Gift

Unwritten Scene unreviewed ~mid-March month after her first solo entrance with the key craft
notes

Vee gifts Pace green bedsheets for his bed, replacing his plain white — her interior color (the panties, the bra) carried into his most private room, one rung past the cabin key: she claims the bed as jointly hers. Pace's switching ritual begins here — green laid on for her, stripped to white for Randi's weeknights, the two sets never in the room together (a reread-detonator that pays off at In Her Place and the scent-erasure that closes it). Warm Vee/Pace beat; the sweetness is the trapdoor the spring Randi/Pace scene rides in on.

Cassie — Spring

Unwritten Vignette unreviewed ~March month Cassandra track
notes

Cassie tries to reconnect; Vee shows up half-present. Cassie says the thing — "She's really involved in your life now, isn't she?" — and Vee has an explanation ready. The reader doesn't. Displacement felt from both sides; the gap is named and dismissed in the same beat.

Secret Plans — The Ask

Architecture complete Scene unreviewed ~mid-late March month Vee POV DIPTYCH (panel 1 of 2) detail craft
notes

The first scene Vee authors. A quiet domestic beat: she invites him — the "adventure" word turned bidirectional at last (she coined it at Still Life, Pace pointed it forward at the cabin; now she uses it to invite). She hands Pace the premise, not a script — shy-brave, native voice, small words (the persona is cover she can want behind, not banter she performs): a space princess chased, caught, stripped, "interrogated" by a galactic overlord for secret plans she'll never reveal. She leaves the "torture" unspecified (you decide); the unreleased plans are the only rule. She schemes the wish; she does not run it. First read: brave, fun, her most confident ask. Reread: the first time she holds the pen she writes herself a cage — her authorship and the machinery that runs her are the same act.

Secret Plans — Pace Builds (off-page, never rendered)

Not a scene Event ~late March month the week after The Ask detail
notes

Making-as-love (rhymes the dress, the key box). He crafts a princess costume that barely covers her at rest and gives her away in motion, seamed with few stitches so it tears off a piece at a time — a gorgeous thing built to come apart (tender AND instrumental).
